symptoms.symptom_name: Single very high pharmacy reading (180/100) with a normal calm home reading (~125/80) after 15 minutes of rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large gap between a high clinical/pharmacy reading and a normal calm home reading is the defining feature of the white-coat effect.
A reading that normalises with rest in the absence of any symptoms is inconsistent with a sustained hypertensive emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing situational anxiety and nervousness specifically around healthcare professionals and medical equipment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A conditioned anxiety response to medical settings and equipment is the mechanism underlying the transient blood-pressure rise in white coat hypertension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Completely asymptomatic - no headache, visual change, chest pain, breathlessness, or focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The complete absence of acute target-organ symptoms strongly argues against a hypertensive emergency and supports a benign process.
An asymptomatic patient with an isolated high reading and normal calm readings fits the benign white-coat picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: No prior diagnosis of hypertension and no regular antihypertensive medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No established hypertension history means this is a first isolated reading requiring out-of-office confirmation rather than an adherence or treatment-titration problem.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular examination (no left ventricular heave), normal fundoscopy (no arteriolar narrowing/silver-wiring/haemorrhages), normal neurological examination (clinician-obtained findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of target-organ damage (no LVH, normal fundoscopy) indicates no chronic hypertensive change and supports a benign, non-sustained process - but requires clinician examination, not obtainable via telehealth chat.
Normal fundoscopy and neurological examination argue against acute target-organ damage that would define a hypertensive emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) / home BP diary (investigation result, pending confirmation at GP follow-up)

investigation_details.investigation_name: 24-hour ambulatory blood pressure monitoring (ABPM) or standardised home BP diary
investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated normal out-of-office blood pressure, confirming white coat hypertension and excluding sustained/masked hypertension - to be arranged at routine GP follow-up, not assumed already complete.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without out-of-office confirmation, genuine sustained or masked hypertension cannot be excluded and the white coat diagnosis remains unconfirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal out-of-office readings confirm white coat hypertension and exclude sustained hypertension; this is the definitive diagnostic step.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A single high clinical/pharmacy reading that normalises with rest, a clear history of clinic-specific anxiety, and a completely asymptomatic patient together support benign white coat hypertension - warranting reassurance and out-of-office confirmation rather than antihypertensive treatment or emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Standardised home blood-pressure monitoring (validated cuff, after 10 minutes quiet rest, recorded in a diary)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The first-line, low-cost way to obtain accurate out-of-office readings, break the clinical-setting anxiety trigger, and provide the GP with a reliable record.
Definitive out-of-office confirmation of white coat hypertension and exclusion of sustained/masked hypertension, arranged via routine GP follow-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Starting antihypertensive medication on the basis of the single high reading
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: An isolated anxiety-driven reading without out-of-office confirmation or target-organ damage should not be treated pharmacologically; doing so over-treats a benign white-coat pattern.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department attendance / urgent bloods and imaging for the isolated reading
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in a completely asymptomatic patient with a normal calm reading; emergency escalation here would be inappropriate over-triage driven by anxiety rather than clinical risk.
